text: for Lateral Fixation: Knotless SwiveLock incorporation If desired, the knotless mechanism from a lateral row Knotless SwiveLock can be used for lateral fixation. If this technique is preferred, do not cinch the FiberLink/ TigerLink SutureTapes when prepping the graft. Use the links as a shuttle to bring the repair stitch through the graft. Convert through the knotless mechanism. For best results, it is important to make sure the graft is placed medially enough to not cover the lateral row anchors, and to make sure that the lateral row anchors are wider than the graft to prevent bunching. CuffMend™ Rotator Cuff Augmentation System | 15 Load one of the lateral SutureTapes through the eyelet of the 3.5 mm self-punching PushLock ® anchor. Taking care to avoid the existing lateral row anchors from the SpeedBridge™ repair construct, introduce the PushLock anchor through the lateral portal and insert the anchor. Repeat this step for the remaining lateral anchor. Note: Take care to not overtension the suture and graft. Lateral Graft Fixation CuffMend™ Rotator Cuff Augmentation System | 17 A 10 mm or larger PassPort Button™ cannula can be placed posteriorly for introduction of the graft spreader. Another PassPort Button cannula can be inserted laterally to be used for viewing, as well as for insertion of PushLock ® anchors. Posterior Approach for CuffMend™ Rotator Cuff Augmentation Portal Placement Graft Sizing Insert the graft spreader into the subacromial space, and roll the wheel forward to deploy. The arm of the graft spreader from the tip to the first bend is approximately 31 mm. Use this approximate measurement as a reference for choosing the appropriately sized ArthroFlex® graft. 23 mm 31 mm Suturing configuration shown for right shoulder 18 | CuffMend™ Rotator Cuff Augmentation System 1 2 3 Using a Scorpion™ suture passer, place a 0.9 mm TigerLink™ SutureTape and a 0.9 mm FiberLink™ SutureTape into the lateral corners of the ArthroFlex dermal allograft in a cinch stitch configuration. Prepare the medial corners of the graft by passing a #0 FiberWire ® suture in a simple pass configuration. Add an additional #0 FiberWire suture to the anterolateral corner of the graft. Note: Orientation of the graft is different for a left shoulder case vs a right shoulder case. The extra #0 FiberWire suture will always go into the anterolateral corner of the graft. Graft Preparation for Posterior Approach With ArthroFLEX® Human Dermal Allograft Note: The SpeedFLEX™ implant is not currently available for the posterior approach. If using a SpeedFLEX implant, sutures will need to be repositioned, and an additional #0 FiberWire ® will be needed. Left Shoulder Suture Configuration A A P P Right Shoulder Suture Configuration CuffMend™ Rotator Cuff Augmentation System | 19 1 Using the suture loader, load all the #0 FiberWire ® sutures through the holes on the graft spreader (a, b, d). 2 Cleat the #0 FiberWire sutures (a, b) in a crisscross pattern to the inside cleats of the graft spreader handle. Cleat the #0 FiberWire suture from corner (d) into (d) on the same side of the graft spreader handle. Cleat both the FiberLink™ and TigerLink™ SutureTapes into (c) on the graft spreader handle. Graft Spreader Loading for Left Shoulder 20 | CuffMend™ Rotator Cuff Augmentation System 1 Using the suture loader, load all the #0 FiberWire ® sutures through the holes on the graft spreader (a, b, c). 2 Cleat the #0 FiberWire sutures (a, b) in a crisscross pattern to the inside cleats of the graft spreader handle. Cleat the #0 FiberWire suture from corner (c) into (c) on the same side of the graft spreader handle. Cleat both the FiberLink™ and TigerLink™ SutureTapes into (d) on the graft spreader handle. Graft Spreader Loading for Right Shoulder CuffMend™ Rotator Cuff Augmentation System | 21 1 Once the graft is loaded onto the graft spreader, retract the articulating arm by sliding the button backward. 2 Introduce the retracted graft spreader through the posterior PassPort Button™ cannula. Once completely introduced into the subacromial space, open the articulating arm by sliding the button on the handle toward the tip of the device, spreading the graft over the desired location on the rotator cuff. Note: The graft spreader will only fit through a 10 mm diameter PassPort Button cannula or larger. Fixate the medial edge of the graft using the FiberStitch RC 1.5 implant. For full details on its use, see pages 9-13. Graft Delivery Medial Graft Fixation 22 | CuffMend™ Rotator Cuff Augmentation System 1 While holding onto the graft spreader, uncleat the FiberLink™ SutureTape and TigerLink™ SutureTape. Retrieve the sutures out of the lateral portal. 2 Uncleat the remaining sutures from the graft spreader handle (see inset). Retract the arm by sliding the button backwards, and then carefully remove the graft spreader from the cannula. Graft Spreader Removal CuffMend™ Rotator
